Messaging

Game developers use messaging to help build player community within their title. Publishers can create messages such as a Message of the Day to be delivered directly to the players of their titles. Other Xbox Live features such as teams and competitions use messaging to help build events and coordinate play. Friend requests and game invitations can include an optional voice attachment to personalize the requests. The messaging framework is flexible and generic enough to pass additional information if a title wants to supplement a message with supporting data. For instance, messages can have voice attachments, text attachments, larger content attachments (such as a supporting graphic image), and so on.

Titles are not required to support the voice or text capabilities in all types of messages. However, the Xbox Live TCRs require that all new games support voice attachments for game invitations and friend requests. If a title chooses to use voice or text for other types of messages, then certain requirements will apply to ensure a consistent Xbox Live experience.

This section presents an overview of messaging in the following topcis.

  • Essential Messaging Concepts
  • Preparing to Send and Receive Messages
  • Creating Messages
  • Sending Messages
  • Receiving Messages
  • Messages with Attachments
  • Messaging Technical Reference
